objective: To explore how task-technology fit influences continuance intention to use digital agricultural information systems among users of CGIAR agricultural information systems in Sub-Saharan Africa.
comments: Refines v1: exploratory framing, CGIAR / digital agricultural IS context, aligned with RQ v4.

objective: To examine the relationship between task-technology fit and continuance intention to use agritech information systems in Sub-Saharan Africa.
comments: Aligned to earlier RQ and core post-adoption focus.

objective: To explore how users evaluate the fit between digital agricultural information systems and the tasks they are required to perform.
comments: Captures task characteristics, technology characteristics, and task–technology fit; allows themes to emerge naturally rather than forcing a predefined TTF measurement model.

objective: To examine how task-technology fit shapes users' post-adoption perceptions, including confirmation of expectations, perceived usefulness, and satisfaction.
comments: ECM enters here. Theoretical logic: TTF → Confirmation → Perceived Usefulness → Satisfaction. Directly justifies integrating TTF into ECM.

objective: To investigate how satisfaction influences users' intention to continue using digital agricultural information systems.
comments: Core ECM relationship: Satisfaction → Continuance Intention. Reinforces why ECM remains the primary theory.

objective: To identify contextual factors that facilitate or constrain the continued use of digital agricultural information systems.
comments: Facilitating conditions. Examples may include connectivity, device availability, training, organizational support, management expectations, and project requirements.

objective: To identify the task and technology characteristics that contribute to task-technology fit.
To explore the role of user satisfaction in the relationship between task-technology fit and continuance intention.
To examine the influence of facilitating conditions on users' ability to continue using agritech information systems.
comments: Adds contextual conditions relevant to SSA settings.
